question: Can I Council the mother to force feed the child or there is any supplements that will bust his immune system and increase his appetite.?
answer:
Please do not force feed the child. This may lead to aspiration of fluid which may lead to a loss of child's life.

Multivitamin syrup can help boost his appetite, however we need to be sure he is having the proper diet.


This is a case of protein energy malnutrition. This patient has a deficit of proteins and energy. There are 3 broad categories of causes:
1. Inadequate intake of protein and calories
2. Malabsorption/non utilization of protein and calories
3. Excessive use off protein and calories.  by the body. This will be seen in high metabolic conditions like cancers, chronic illnesses like tuberculosis.

It is important to trace the likely cause by taking a history and examining this child. Some investigations like stool  MCS may also reveal parasitic conditions like an cyclist omits is that prevent absorption of nutrients. 
It is pertinent to say however, that the most common cause especially in resource poor settings like ours is inadequate intake due to poverty, lack or education or awareness, frequent conflicts and displacements. This patient may not be taking adequately because he is sick and has reduced appetite.

Plan
This patient will need a multidisciplinary management by paediatricians, dieticians and nutritionists.

Counsel parents on the need to commence high protein and energy diets.
Sometimes ready-to-use-therapeutic-foods (RUTF) are provided by the government and some foreign organizations. In this case, you will contact the relevant officers for these products which have been shown to be highly beneficial.

In the absence of RUTF, let caregivers know that this patient will need to be fed every two hours with high protein foods which are locally available in the form of fish, egg, meat, liver, milk, beans, cheese, groundnuts.
The staple diets high in carbohydrates should be enriched with these protein foods and vegetables and fruits. If possible, make a diet timetable for the week with the aid of a dietician and hand to these parents.

Where social works are available, kindly notify them if the patient lacks the finances to fund this diet plan.

question: Me zan bashi?
answer: History should be taken to determine onset and duration of symptom while ruling out presence of other systemic symptoms. One should ask about patient's diet, household source of drinking water, if patient sleeps under a mosquito net, food hygiene and hand hygiene practices, and what remedies may have been used before presentation. A physical examination should be done while looking out for fever, pallor, dehydration, tenderness of the ears and inflammation in the throat among others. A blood sample should be taken for malaria parasite smear and full blood count. Patient should receive antipyretic medication for the fever and should subsequently be reviewed with investigation results. In this case, patient was found to have malaria on investigation. Appropriate medications should then be prescribed including an ACT drug(Artemisinin based Combination Therapy) for the malaria.

target_text: A woman came to the hospital yesterday with her child, who is 5 months and 4 days old, saying he has been having a fever and they haven't slept for 3 days because his body is hot. When I checked the child's temperature, I found it was very high, at 38.7 °C, indicating a significant fever. I asked her what medication she had given him, and she said they went to the chemist and were given paracetamol syrup, which she administered to him. After taking it, his body would cool down for a short while, but then it would become hot again, which is why she brought him in. I then sent them to get a malaria test and a typhoid test. In fact, the malaria test showed 2+ for malaria in the child's body, indicating a significant presence of malaria.
reference: History should be taken to determine onset and duration of symptom while ruling out presence of other systemic symptoms. One should ask about patient's diet, household source of drinking water, if patient sleeps under a mosquito net, food hygiene and hand hygiene practices, and what remedies may have been used before presentation. A physical examination should be done while looking out for fever, pallor, dehydration, tenderness of the ears and inflammation in the throat among others. A blood sample should be taken for malaria parasite smear and full blood count. Patient should receive antipyretic medication for the fever and should subsequently be reviewed with investigation results. In this case, patient was found to have malaria on investigation. Appropriate medications should then be prescribed including an ACT drug(Artemisinin based Combination Therapy) for the malaria.

question: Menene zan musu
answer:
Thank you for your questions.
This case is not what you should keep and manage in your facility at the PHC level. He will require an abdominopelvic ultrasound scan, CT scan or even an MRI. Depending on the tissue injured, he might need an urgent exploratory laparotomy. This can be done in a secondary or tertiary health facility.
